core_payload.content:
# Agent Context Engineering

## 1. Definition and Essence
Agent Context Engineering defines the discipline of constructing the right context for an agent run instead of throwing a whole vault, transcript, or tool log into one prompt. It treats context as a designed artifact: selected, compressed, branch-aware, and edited for the specific job at hand.

## 2. Core Responsibilities
- `context_packs` assemble bounded neighborhoods around a target capsule, graph signal, or planning question.
- `memory_stratification` separates working context, session summary, long-term memory, and durable artifacts instead of mixing them together.
- `artifact_separation` keeps files, plans, traces, and job outputs outside the main prompt when they can be referenced more cheaply and cleanly.
- `context_editing` trims stale tool noise, repetitive history, and irrelevant graph branches before the run starts.
- `instruction_surface_injection` ensures repo doctrine and workflow law are injected deliberately instead of appearing only through accidental prior chat turns.
- `retrieval_budgeting` limits how much vault evidence, history, and branch context each lane may consume before the run becomes noisy or too expensive.
- `branch_awareness` ensures Dream and Real context are explicitly labeled rather than silently blended.
- `handoff_ready_context` produces portable bundles that can be passed from scout to foreman, reviewer, executor, or another lane without losing structure.

## 3. N1Hub Role
In N1Hub this pattern is essential for Chat to Capsules, Personal AI Assistant, DeepMine, Symphony, and Vault Stewardship Swarm. A swarm that sees the whole vault without context engineering wastes tokens, drifts, and hallucinates priorities. A swarm that receives carefully designed context packs can study one capsule neighborhood deeply, compare it with recent run history, see the relevant instruction surfaces, and return a sharper plan or bounded update.

## 4. Architectural Value
Context engineering improves quality, cost, and recoverability at the same time. It creates smaller prompts, cleaner handoffs, stronger reproducibility, and better debugging because every run can explain why it saw the evidence it saw, which doctrine framed it, and which evidence was left out.

## 5. Operational Rule
Deep work does not mean maximum context. It means the minimum context that still preserves the right truth, branch, evidence, and doctrine for the job.

## 6. Philosophical Alignment
Agent Context Engineering supports To Dig Deep by making deep work come from careful evidence selection and context design instead of raw prompt volume.

neuro_concentrate.summary: Agent Context Engineering defines the practice of building bounded context packs for agent work instead of dumping entire transcripts or vault snapshots into a single prompt. It separates working context from long-term memory and durable artifacts, keeps Dream and Real explicit, injects instruction surfaces deliberately, and makes handoff-ready bundles that can move cleanly between scout, foreman, reviewer, and executor lanes. For N1Hub this matters because swarm quality depends less on seeing everything and more on seeing the right capsule neighborhood, doctrine, branch state, and recent history at the right time.
